name: Customer Complaint Resolution Process
description: The end-to-end operational workflow for managing and resolving customer complaints at FirstUK Bank. This process is owned by the Head of Customer Service and involves both Customer Service and Compliance departments.
context: FirstUK Bank — Customer Complaint Resolution Guide

name: Head of Customer Service
description: The designated owner of the Customer Complaint Resolution Guide, responsible for the overall governance of the complaint process.

name: Customer Service Department
description: The primary department responsible for the execution and management of customer complaint resolutions.

name: Compliance Department
description: The department providing regulatory oversight and support for the complaint resolution process to ensure adherence to banking standards.

name: Support Staff
description: The frontline personnel who are the intended audience for the guide and responsible for interacting with customers regarding complaints.

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who initiates a complaint and is the primary subject of the resolution process.

name: Customer Complaint Resolution Process
description: An end-to-end procedure designed to help customers understand how to raise complaints, what to expect during the process, and the available paths for escalation if they remain unsatisfied.
context: Help customers understand the complaint process and their rights
how to raise complaints, what to expect, and how to escalate if unsatisfied

name: Customer
description: The individual who initiates the complaint process to exercise their rights and seek resolution for an issue.
context: Help customers understand the complaint process

name: Complaint Resolution Process
description: The end-to-end procedure for managing expressions of dissatisfaction regarding FirstUK Bank services, products, or actions that require a formal response.
context: A complaint is any expression of dissatisfaction about our service, products, or actions that requires a response.

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who expresses dissatisfaction regarding a service, product, or action, such as failed transfers or poor branch treatment.
context: "I wasn't informed about a rate change"
"I was treated poorly in a branch"

name: Failed Transfer Transaction
description: A financial transfer that did not complete successfully and has not been refunded, triggering a customer complaint.

name: Unauthorized Direct Debit
description: A Direct Debit transaction taken from a customer account without the required authorization, cited as a reason for a complaint.
context: "A Direct Debit was taken without authorization"

name: Complaint Resolution Process
description: The end-to-end procedure for handling customer complaints, featuring an 8-day response guarantee and a path to external escalation via the Financial Ombudsman Service.
context: We will respond to your complaint within 8 calendar days of receipt.
If you're unhappy with our response, you can escalate to the Financial Ombudsman Service

name: Complaint Investigation
description: A thorough investigation of the customer's complaint to provide a clear explanation of the findings.
context: We will thoroughly investigate your complaint and provide a clear explanation.

name: Complaint Response
description: The act of responding to the customer's complaint within a strict timeline of 8 calendar days from the date of receipt.
context: We will respond to your complaint within 8 calendar days of receipt.

name: Response Satisfaction Check
description: A decision point where the customer determines if they are satisfied with the bank's response; if unhappy, the process moves to external escalation.
context: If you're unhappy with our response, you can escalate to the Financial Ombudsman Service

name: External Escalation — Financial Ombudsman Service
description: The final escalation tier involving a free and independent external body for customers unhappy with the bank's internal resolution.
context: you can escalate to the Financial Ombudsman Service (free and independent).

name: Financial Ombudsman Service
description: A free and independent external body responsible for reviewing complaints escalated by customers.
context: escalate to the Financial Ombudsman Service (free and independent).

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has submitted a complaint and is entitled to a response within 8 days and protection from retaliation.
context: We will respond to your complaint
We will not penalize you for making a complaint.

name: Complaint Raising Process
description: The end-to-end procedure for customers to initiate a complaint with FirstUK Bank. This specific section covers the telephone channel for reporting issues.

name: Telephone Complaint Initiation
description: The initial step where a customer contacts the bank via phone (0800-555-2265) during specified operating hours to explain their issue.

name: Customer
description: The individual initiating the complaint process by calling the bank and explaining their issue.

name: Supervisor
description: A higher-level staff member who can be requested by the customer to handle the complaint if the initial interaction is insufficient.
context: ask to speak with a supervisor if needed

name: Supervisor Escalation Trigger
description: A decision point where the customer determines if the current level of service is sufficient or if they need to escalate the matter to a supervisor.
context: ask to speak with a supervisor if needed

name: Formal Complaint Lodgement Process (Email Channel)
description: The procedure for customers to lodge a formal complaint via email to the bank's support address. It specifies the required subject line and the mandatory information the customer must provide to initiate the resolution process.
context: I want to lodge a formal complaint

name: Customer
description: The individual initiating the formal complaint process by sending an email to the bank's support address with their account details and issue description.

name: Bank Support Team
description: The recipient of the formal complaint email, responsible for receiving the customer's submission at the designated support email address.

name: Complaint Email Submission
description: The action where the customer sends an email to support@firstuk.bank with the subject 'I want to lodge a formal complaint', including their name, account number, date of issue, description of events, perceived error, and desired resolution.
context: Subject: "I want to lodge a formal complaint"

name: Customer Account
description: The specific bank account associated with the customer lodging the complaint, identified by the account number provided in the email.

name: Branch-Based Complaint Resolution Process
description: A process allowing customers to lodge complaints by visiting any of the 12 UK bank branches. The process involves interacting with management or utilizing formal documentation.
context: Locations: Any of our 12 UK branches
What to Do: Ask to speak with a manager or use the complaint form.

name: Request Manager Consultation
aliases: Ask to speak with a manager
description: The action where a customer requests a direct conversation with a branch manager to address their complaint.
context: Ask to speak with a manager

name: Complaint Form Submission
description: The action where a customer utilizes a formal complaint form to document their grievance at a branch location.

name: Branch Manager
description: The staff member at the branch responsible for speaking with customers regarding their complaints.
context: Ask to speak with a manager

name: Complaining Customer
description: The individual visiting a UK branch to initiate a complaint via a manager or a form.
context: Ask to speak with a manager or use the complaint form

name: Online Complaint Submission Process
description: A digital channel process allowing customers to lodge complaints via a secure online form hosted on the bank's official website.
context: Process: Fill out form and submit securely.

name: Online Complaint Form Completion and Submission
description: The action where the user populates the required fields in the online complaint form and submits the data securely through the website.
context: Fill out form and submit securely.

name: Customer
description: The individual initiating the complaint process by accessing the website and submitting the online form.
context: Fill out form and submit securely.

name: Complaint Handling Process
description: A four-step operational workflow for managing customer complaints, beginning with an immediate acknowledgment phase to provide the customer with tracking details and investigator information.
context: Within 1 business day of receipt, you'll receive

name: Complaint Acknowledgment Step
description: The initial stage of the complaint process where the bank acknowledges receipt of the complaint within 1 business day. This step involves issuing a reference number and providing the name of the assigned investigator.
context: Within 1 business day of receipt, you'll receive: Complaint Reference Number
Acknowledgment Email/Letter confirming we've received your complaint

name: Complaint Investigator
description: The individual responsible for investigating the customer's complaint, whose name is provided to the customer during the acknowledgment phase.

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has submitted a complaint and is the recipient of the acknowledgment and reference number.

name: Investigation Stage
description: A discrete stage occurring between Day 1 and Day 7 where the investigation team reviews records, interviews staff, and contacts third parties to determine the cause of an issue and the necessity of compensation.
context: Our investigation team will: Review all relevant documents and records

name: Investigation Team
description: The internal team responsible for reviewing documents, interviewing staff, contacting third parties, and assessing the need for compensation during the investigation stage.
context: Review all relevant documents and records

name: Involved Staff
description: Bank personnel who were involved in the event under investigation and may be interviewed by the investigation team.

name: Third Parties
description: External entities, such as merchants or other banking institutions, that may be contacted by the investigation team for information.
context: Contact third parties (if needed, e.g., merchant, other bank)

name: Customer
description: The subject of the process who may be contacted for additional information during the investigation period.
context: You may be contacted for additional information if needed.

name: Compensation Warranted Decision
description: A decision point at the end of the investigation to determine whether the findings justify providing compensation to the customer.

name: Complaint Resolution Process
description: An operational workflow for handling customer complaints, including an investigation phase and a formal decision phase. The process involves issuing a response letter to the customer by Day 8 of the specific stage.
context: You'll receive a response letter explaining

name: Complaint Decision and Notification Step
description: The final stage of the current process phase where the bank communicates its findings, the decision on the complaint, the resulting action, and any awarded compensation to the customer via a response letter. This must be completed by Day 8.
context: You'll receive a response letter explaining: - Our findings (what we discovered) - Our decision (upheld, partially upheld, or rejected) - Our action (refund, explanation, other resolution) - Compensation (if awarded)

name: Complaint Outcome Decision
description: A branching point where the bank determines if a complaint is upheld, partially upheld, or rejected. This decision dictates the subsequent action, such as a refund or a detailed explanation.
context: Our decision (upheld, partially upheld, or rejected)

name: Complaint Handling Officer
description: The internal bank actor or team responsible for conducting findings, making the final decision on the complaint, and issuing the response letter to the customer.
context: Our decision (upheld, partially upheld, or rejected)

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has submitted a complaint and is the recipient of the response letter detailing the bank's findings and decision.
context: You'll receive a response letter explaining

name: Complaint Resolution and Escalation Process
description: The final stage of the complaint handling workflow focusing on the resolution of the issue and the customer's options for escalation if the bank's decision is not accepted.
context: If you disagree with our decision

name: Complaint Closure and Refund Processing
description: The action taken when a customer accepts the bank's decision, resulting in the formal closure of the complaint and the processing of any applicable refunds.

name: External Escalation to Financial Ombudsman
description: The process where a customer who disagrees with the bank's decision refers the matter to the Financial Ombudsman, which can be done within 6 months.
context: You have 6 months to escalate to Financial Ombudsman
You can escalate anytime without further correspondence with us

name: Customer
description: The individual who has raised a complaint and must decide whether to accept the bank's resolution or escalate the matter externally.
context: If you disagree with our decision

name: Financial Ombudsman
description: The external regulatory body responsible for reviewing complaints that have not been resolved to the customer's satisfaction by the bank.

name: Decision Acceptance Gate
description: A branching point based on whether the customer accepts or disagrees with the bank's decision, leading either to complaint closure or external escalation.
context: If you disagree with our decision

name: External Tier — Financial Ombudsman
description: The final escalation tier outside of the bank's internal structure, accessible to the customer within a 6-month window.

name: Failed Transfer Complaint Resolution Process
description: An operational workflow to investigate and resolve customer complaints regarding failed fund transfers where money was debited from the account. The process aims to determine the cause of failure and provide a resolution within 2–3 business days.

name: Transfer Status Investigation
description: A diagnostic step to determine if the transfer completed or failed, whether funds were debited, and the root cause of the failure.
context: Did the transfer actually complete or fail?
If failed, was the money debited or not?

name: Transfer Failure Root Cause Decision
description: A branching point based on the investigation outcome to determine the resolution path: system error (Authorized), Insufficient funds, or Invalid recipient.
context: Authorized: No fee charged (system error); refund + interest
Insufficient funds: Explain why it failed; no refund
Invalid recipient: Explain error; refund if our fault

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has experienced a failed transfer and is seeking a refund of debited funds.
context: "I tried to transfer £500 but it failed. Money was taken from my account."

name: Failed Transfer Transaction
description: The specific financial event involving a £500 transfer that failed to complete but resulted in funds being debited from the customer's account.
context: I tried to transfer £500 but it failed.
Money was taken from my account.

name: Customer Account
description: The customer account from which the failed transfer funds were debited.
context: Money was taken from my account.

name: Wrong Interest Charged Investigation Process
description: An operational workflow to investigate and resolve customer complaints regarding incorrect interest rates. The process involves comparing expected versus actual rates and determining the cause of the error, with a typical resolution timeline of 3–5 business days.

name: Interest Rate Discrepancy Investigation
description: The analytical phase of the process where the bank determines the correct rate, the actual rate charged, the duration of the discrepancy, and the financial impact on the customer.
context: What rate were you supposed to receive?
What rate were you actually charged?
Did this benefit or harm you?

name: Interest Error Root Cause Determination
description: A decision gate to categorize the investigation outcome into one of three paths: bank error, policy error, or no error, which dictates the subsequent resolution action.
context: Our error: Recalculate interest; refund difference + additional interest for inconvenience
Policy error: Explain policy; refund if error caused loss
No error: Explain the rate and why it's correct

name: Bank Error Remediation
description: The action taken when a bank error is identified, involving the recalculation of interest and the issuance of a refund including compensation for inconvenience.
context: Our error: Recalculate interest; refund difference + additional interest for inconvenience

name: Policy Error Resolution
description: The action taken when a policy error is identified, involving an explanation of the policy to the customer and a refund if the error resulted in a financial loss.
context: Policy error: Explain policy; refund if error caused loss

name: No Error Clarification
description: The action taken when no error is found, requiring the bank to explain the applied rate and justify why it is correct.
context: No error: Explain the rate and why it's correct

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has raised an issue regarding being charged the wrong interest rate.
context: Your Issue: "I was charged the wrong interest rate."
What rate were you supposed to receive?

name: Unauthorized Direct Debit Investigation Process
description: An operational workflow to investigate customer claims of unauthorized Direct Debit withdrawals. The process involves verifying mandates and authorization, with a typical resolution timeline of 5–10 business days.

name: Direct Debit Mandate and Authorization Investigation
description: A review stage where the bank investigates the existence of a signed mandate, the fact of authorization, the timing of authorization, and the accuracy of the amount taken.
context: Is the amount correct per mandate?

name: Direct Debit Authorization Outcome Decision
description: A branching point based on the investigation results to determine if the transaction was genuinely unauthorized, authorized but forgotten, or an amount error.
context: Genuinely unauthorized: Refund £150 + interest within 10 days
You authorized but forgot: Explanation; option to cancel going forward

name: Bank Investigator
description: The internal bank entity responsible for investigating the Direct Debit mandate and determining the outcome of the claim.

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has reported an unauthorized Direct Debit withdrawal and is seeking a refund.
context: I didn't authorize this Direct Debit, and £150 was taken.

name: Disputed Direct Debit Transaction
description: The specific financial event of £150 being taken via Direct Debit which is the subject of the investigation.

name: Direct Debit Mandate
description: The authorization agreement used to verify if the customer permitted the Direct Debit withdrawal.
context: Is the amount correct per mandate?

name: Poor Customer Service Complaint Process
description: An operational workflow to investigate and resolve complaints regarding rude staff behavior in branches. The process involves investigating the incident details and concludes with a resolution within 5–7 business days.

name: Complaint Investigation
description: The stage where the bank gathers specific details regarding the incident, including the timing, the staff member involved, the nature of the interaction, and the impact on the customer.

name: Complaint Outcome Determination
description: A decision gate where the investigation results are categorized into one of three paths: service failure confirmed, misunderstanding, or not substantiated, each leading to a different resolution action.

name: Service Failure Resolution
description: The action taken when a service failure is confirmed, consisting of an apology, staff retraining, and a compensation payment between £20 and £100 based on severity.
context: Service failure confirmed: Apology; staff retraining; compensation (£20–£100 depending on severity)

name: Misunderstanding Clarification
description: The action taken when the investigation determines the issue was a misunderstanding, involving an explanation of the staff member's actions and a clarification.
context: Misunderstanding: Explanation of staff's actions; clarification

name: Unsubstantiated Finding Notification
description: The action taken when the complaint is not substantiated, resulting in an explanation of the bank's findings to the customer.
context: Not substantiated: Explanation of our findings

name: Branch Staff Member
description: The employee in the branch who is the subject of the customer's complaint regarding rude behavior.
context: Staff member was rude to me in the branch.

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has experienced poor service in a branch and is the subject of the investigation.
context: Staff member was rude to me in the branch.

name: Account Restrictions Investigation Process
description: An operational workflow to investigate and resolve customer complaints regarding frozen accounts. The process determines the justification for the freeze and defines outcomes based on whether the action was regulatory, fraud-related, or a process error, with a typical resolution time of 3–5 business days.

name: Freeze Justification Investigation
description: The initial investigative stage where the bank determines the reason for the account freeze, checks if proper notice was provided to the customer, and evaluates if the freeze was justified.
context: Why was it frozen? (Regulatory reason? Fraud hold? Other?)

name: Freeze Justification Decision
description: A branching point based on the investigation outcome. Paths lead to 'Justified' (explain reason and timeline), 'Unjustified' (immediate unfreeze and compensation), or 'Process error' (unfreeze and process improvement).
context: Justified: Explain reason (e.g., KYC compliance, fraud investigation); timeline for resolution

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has experienced an account freeze without explanation and is seeking resolution.

name: Frozen Account
description: The specific customer account that has been restricted or frozen, which is the subject of the investigation.
context: My account was frozen without explanation.

name: KYC Compliance Check
description: A regulatory gate used as a potential justification for freezing an account to ensure compliance with Know Your Customer requirements.
context: Explain reason (e.g., KYC compliance, fraud investigation)

name: Fraud Hold Investigation
description: The process of investigating a fraud-related hold placed on an account to determine if the restriction is justified.
context: Explain reason (e.g., KYC compliance, fraud investigation)

name: Escalation to Financial Ombudsman Service
description: The operational workflow for escalating a customer dispute to the Financial Ombudsman Service. This process is triggered when a customer is dissatisfied with a decision or the bank fails to meet communication timelines.

name: Ombudsman Escalation Trigger Check
description: A decision gate to determine if a case qualifies for escalation to the Financial Ombudsman. Qualification is based on customer dissatisfaction, a lack of response within 8 days, or failure to acknowledge receipt.

name: External Regulator — Financial Ombudsman Service
description: The final external escalation tier where disputes are referred for independent resolution after internal bank processes have failed or timelines have been breached.

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who initiates the escalation process to the Financial Ombudsman Service due to dissatisfaction or service failure.

name: Complaint Escalation to Financial Ombudsman Service
description: The end-to-end procedure for a customer to escalate a dispute to the Financial Ombudsman Service after receiving a decision from FirstUK. It involves providing specific documentation and contact details for the external regulator.

name: Financial Ombudsman Service
description: The external regulatory body responsible for reviewing complaints escalated by customers who disagree with FirstUK's decision.

name: External Escalation — Financial Ombudsman Service
description: The final tier of escalation outside of the bank's internal processes, where the Financial Ombudsman Service reviews the case based on the bank's decision letter and customer evidence.
context: Clear explanation of why you disagree

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who is initiating the escalation process by providing their reference number and decision letter to the Ombudsman.

name: Financial Ombudsman Complaint Resolution Process
description: An independent external review process conducted by the Ombudsman to resolve customer complaints. The process typically takes 3–6 months and can result in a binding decision or compensation award.
context: The Ombudsman will: Review your complaint independently

name: Financial Ombudsman
description: An independent external body responsible for reviewing complaints, contacting the bank for information, and issuing decisions or compensation awards.
context: The Ombudsman will: Review your complaint independently

name: FirstUK Bank
description: The banking institution subject to the Ombudsman's review and bound by the final decision issued by the Ombudsman.
context: Issue a decision (binding on us, non-binding on you)

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has submitted a complaint for independent review and for whom the Ombudsman's decision is non-binding.

name: Independent Complaint Review
description: The initial stage where the Ombudsman independently reviews the details of the customer's complaint.

name: Information Request from Bank
description: The step where the Ombudsman contacts FirstUK to gather additional information necessary for the case review.

name: Decision Issuance
description: The formal issuance of a decision by the Ombudsman, which is binding on FirstUK but non-binding on the customer.
context: Issue a decision (binding on us, non-binding on you)

name: Compensation Award
description: The final action of granting compensation to the customer if the Ombudsman agrees with the complaint.
context: Make an award (compensation) if they agree with you

name: Ombudsman Agreement Gate
description: A decision point where the Ombudsman determines if they agree with the customer's complaint, triggering a compensation award if successful.
context: Make an award (compensation) if they agree with you

name: Complaint Compensation Process
description: The operational procedure for determining and offering remedies to customers following a complaint. It encompasses both financial compensation and non-financial remedies.

name: FirstUK Bank Compensation Officer
description: The implicit actor responsible for offering financial compensation and non-financial remedies to the complaining customer.

name: Complaining Customer
description: The bank customer who has raised a complaint and is eligible to receive financial or non-financial remedies.

name: Financial Compensation Provision
description: The stage of the process where financial remedies are determined, including refunds for loss, interest for delays, and goodwill payments ranging from £20 to £100+.

name: Non-Financial Remedy Provision
description: The stage of the process where non-monetary resolutions are provided, such as apologies, explanations, and commitments to service or policy improvements.

name: Customer Compensation Process
description: A procedure for determining the appropriate financial compensation range for customers based on the severity of the issue, ranging from minor inconveniences to major service failures.

name: Issue Severity Assessment
description: A decision point where the severity of a customer issue is categorized as Minor, Moderate, Significant, or Major to determine the corresponding compensation range.

name: Financial Ombudsman Service
description: An external regulatory body whose guidance is used to determine compensation amounts for Major severity issues.

name: Complaint Resolution Process
description: The end-to-end procedure for handling customer complaints, ensuring a free lodgement process, a response within 8 calendar days, and a thorough investigation leading to a detailed explanation and potential compensation.
context: No charge to lodge or escalate

name: Complaint Lodgement
description: The initial stage where a customer submits a complaint. This action is provided free of charge.
context: No charge to lodge or escalate

name: Full Investigation
description: A comprehensive review of the customer's issue to determine the facts and findings of the complaint.

name: Findings Explanation
description: The step where the bank provides a detailed explanation of the investigation findings to the customer.
context: We explain our findings in detail

name: Compensation Payment
description: The action of providing financial compensation to the customer if the bank is found to be in error.
context: If we're wrong, we compensate you

name: Customer
description: The individual lodging the complaint who is entitled to a free process, a response within 8 days, and the right to appeal to the Ombudsman.
context: You have 6 months to escalate to Ombudsman
We won't penalize you for complaining

name: FirstUK Bank
description: The entity responsible for responding to complaints within 8 days, conducting investigations, and providing explanations or compensation.
context: We explain our findings in detail
If we're wrong, we compensate you

name: Financial Ombudsman
description: The external body to which a customer can escalate their complaint if they are unhappy with the bank's resolution.
context: Escalate to Financial Ombudsman if unhappy
You have 6 months to escalate to Ombudsman

name: Resolution Satisfaction Gate
description: A decision point where the customer determines if they are satisfied with the bank's findings; if unhappy, the process escalates to the Financial Ombudsman.
context: Escalate to Financial Ombudsman if unhappy

name: External Escalation — Financial Ombudsman
description: The final tier of escalation outside the bank, accessible within a 6-month window following the bank's response.
context: Escalate to Financial Ombudsman if unhappy
You have 6 months to escalate to Ombudsman

name: Complaint Prevention Procedure
description: A set of preventative guidelines and best practices designed to help customers avoid operational issues that lead to formal complaints. It focuses on data verification, monitoring, and security hygiene.

name: Transfer Detail Verification
description: The action of verifying account numbers and sort codes prior to initiating financial transfers to ensure accuracy.
context: Verify details before making transfers (account number, sort code)

name: Communication Record Keeping
description: The practice of maintaining a comprehensive history of all interactions and communications with the bank.

name: Account Activity Monitoring
description: The regular review of account statements and transactions to identify and flag unauthorized activity.
context: Monitor your account regularly for unauthorized activity

name: Rapid Issue Reporting
description: The prompt notification of the bank regarding any identified issues to prevent delays in resolution.
context: Report issues quickly — don't wait weeks

name: Security Credential Management
description: The implementation of secure passwords and the activation of Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) to protect account access.

name: Proactive Inquiry
description: The act of questioning anomalies or suspicious elements in banking services before they escalate into complaints.
context: Ask questions if something seems wrong

name: Customer
description: The primary actor responsible for following the prevention tips, monitoring their own account, and reporting issues to the bank.

name: Complaint Process
description: The end-to-end procedure for handling customer complaints at FirstUK Bank, including support channels for inquiries and a path for escalation to the Financial Ombudsman.
context: For Questions About the Complaint Process

name: Financial Ombudsman
description: An external regulatory body to which complaints can be escalated for independent resolution.

name: External Escalation — Financial Ombudsman
description: The final tier of escalation for complaints that have not been resolved internally, involving the Financial Ombudsman Service.

name: Complaint Resolution Process
description: An operational workflow designed to handle and fix customer issues to ensure satisfaction and drive service improvement.

name: Head of Customer Service
description: The individual responsible for authoring the initial version of the complaint resolution guide.
